key_departures.impact: Mays started 12 games in 2025 and was the incumbent center entering free agency. He signed a 3-year, $25M deal ($14M gtd) with Detroit. His departure immediately created a starting hole at center that the Panthers addressed with Luke Fortner (1-year bridge deal).

Dowdle posted back-to-back 1,000-yard rushing seasons, including with Carolina in 2025. His production was a key factor in the Panthers' NFC South title run. Departure leaves a void as the clear lead back; Chuba Hubbard, Jonathan Brooks, and Trevor Etienne remain but none has proven to be a true workhorse replacement.

Released by Carolina to save $10.5M in cap space. Robinson was a two-year rotational starter who logged 65 tackles, 2.5 sacks, and 8 QB hits in 2025. His release frees cap but thins the IDL rotation behind Derrick Brown.

Nijman announced his retirement on March 11. He was a depth tackle who served as the primary backup behind Ikem Ekwonu. With Ekwonu recovering from a ruptured patellar tendon and timeline unclear, losing Nijman to retirement escalates OT depth need. Panthers pivoted quickly with Stone Forsythe.
Rozeboom was a backup and special teams contributor. His departure is minor given the Devin Lloyd signing, but he was previously the primary depth LB. Low impact on need picture.
Depth safety allowed to reach free agency. Minimal impact on starting lineup.
Rotational EDGE rusher allowed to reach free agency. Panthers largely replaced his role (and exceeded it) with the Jaelan Phillips signing. Minimal net impact.
Depth EDGE rusher. Departure is low impact given Phillips addition.
Depth corner. Minimal impact given Horn-led secondary and Evans re-sign.

signings.analysis: Landmark signing — the largest free-agent EDGE deal in franchise history. Phillips had 61 QB pressures (11th in the NFL) in 2025 split between Miami and Philadelphia. The 4-year/$120M deal with $80M guaranteed is a clear long-term investment at a position of critical need. The Panthers were last in the NFL in pressure rate (26.2%) in 2025 and tied for 28th in sacks. This fully addresses the EDGE need at starter level with elite guarantees. At $30M AAV, this is above the $25M+ elite market tier for EDGE. EDGE drops from a tier-1 critical need to a tier-4 luxury/depth consideration in the draft.

Second major Day 1 signing. Lloyd earned second-team All-Pro honors in 2025 after a career-high 5 interceptions (tied 2nd in the NFL) and 81 tackles. The 3-year/$45M deal with $25M guaranteed represents a fully committed starter investment at $15M AAV — which ties for 5th among LBs per Spotrac. GM Dan Morgan had publicly promised to 'attack' the LB position. Lloyd fulfills that completely. He's expected to serve as the defensive signal-caller, the role Carolina has lacked since Luke Kuechly. LB drops from a tier-1 critical need to resolved/tier-5.

One-year deal to serve as Bryce Young's backup and replace aging Andy Dalton (38). Pickett is a former first-round pick with 27 NFL starts across Steelers, Eagles, Browns, and Raiders stints. At $7.5M/$4M guaranteed on a 1-year deal, this is a bridge/compete deal — he's not a long-term solution. QB depth need is partially addressed in that a capable veteran backup is now in place, but the Panthers should still monitor QB depth in later draft rounds. QB1 position is clearly Bryce Young's. Backup need moves from tier-2 to tier-3.

Signed immediately after Cade Mays departed to Detroit to plug the starting center hole. Fortner started 10 games for the Saints in 2025 after Erik McCoy's injury, allowing QB pressure on only 4.3% of pass-blocking snaps (8th-lowest among qualifying centers). He's a capable veteran stopgap. However, this is a 1-year deal with reported minimal guarantees, which signals a bridge/placeholder — not a franchise answer. The Panthers are expected to draft a center for long-term development. IOL need remains elevated from a draft perspective (tier-2 still warranted).

Signed as swing tackle depth to cover for Ikem Ekwonu's uncertain recovery timeline from a ruptured patellar tendon. Forsythe has 27 career starts and familiarity with head coach Dave Canales from their shared time in Seattle. At $2M/year with $1.05M guaranteed, this is clearly depth insurance. He graded poorly in Las Vegas (55.2 PFF, 78th out of 89 qualifying tackles). OT remains a critical draft need — picking a tackle as high as 19th overall is possible. This signing does not move the needle on OT need.

Low-cost depth/familiarity signing. Metchie was Young's college teammate at Alabama (96 catches, 1,142 yards, 8 TDs together in 2021). He had 33 catches for 274 yards in 2025 split between Philadelphia and New York. One-year deal at minimum-tier money; he projects as WR4 behind Tetairoa McMillan, Jalen Coker, and Xavier Legette. WR was not a primary need entering free agency and this doesn't change the calculus.

Re-signed on a 1-year deal. Evans played 17 games in 2025 as a special teams stalwart (37.7% of all ST snaps) and occasional CB depth behind Jaycee Horn. This is a continuity/special teams retention, not a starter-level investment. CB was not a pressing pre-FA need with Horn entrenched.

Re-signed on a short-term deal. Simmons is a versatile LB/S hybrid who appeared in 5 games in 2025 after being claimed off waivers from the Vikings. At minimum/near-minimum money, this is a depth/flexibility retention for Ejiro Evero's defense. S was not a primary need.

Restricted free agent re-signed on a low-cost deal. Ray started 9 games in 2024 filling in for Derrick Brown and provided rotational value in 2025 (65 tackles, 2.5 sacks over two years). His re-sign provides some IDL depth continuity after A'Shawn Robinson's release. Minimally offsets the Robinson departure at IDL.
Re-signed as a depth/special teams CB. Rochell is a journeyman who has appeared in 18 games (1 start) as a Panther, primarily on special teams. Minimal positional impact.

Restricted free agent re-signed as depth EDGE/special teams contributor. Appeared in 18 games with 1 start after being claimed off waivers from the Vikings in 2024. EDGE need was fully addressed by Phillips; this is depth continuity.

Veteran depth WR re-signed at minimum-tier money. Moore is a locker room veteran and occasional contributor. WR depth was not a primary need with McMillan, Coker, Legette, and now Metchie all returning.
Franchise record 18th season for Jansen, the franchise's all-time leader in games played (277). Routine annual re-signing at long snapper. No positional need implications.

recommended_need_adjustments.reasoning: Jaelan Phillips 4yr/$120M ($80M gtd) at $30M AAV is an elite-market commitment to a 26-year-old with 61 QB pressures in 2025. Carolina went from last in the NFL in pressure rate to having a franchise cornerstone pass rusher locked up for 4 years. EDGE is no longer a draft need at any meaningful tier. Depth rotation (Thomas Incoom re-signed, DJ Wonnum departed) could use a Day 3 addition but this is not a priority.

Devin Lloyd 3yr/$45M ($25M gtd) signing is the best off-ball LB available in 2026 free agency by most rankings. 2x All-Pro, 5 INTs in 2025, serves as the defensive captain/signal-caller. GM Dan Morgan fulfilled his combine promise to 'attack' this position. The starter problem is solved definitively. Isaiah Simmons re-sign adds flexibility depth. No draft investment needed at LB.

Cade Mays (3yr/$25M Lions) departure created a new need, quickly patched with Luke Fortner on a 1-year/$4.75M bridge deal. Fortner is a capable veteran starter (ranked 17th among qualifying centers by PFF in 2025) but the 1-year structure signals the team is not committed long-term. They should strongly target a center in rounds 2-4 of the draft for multi-year development. IOL need unchanged in draft urgency.

Ikem Ekwonu's ruptured patellar tendon has an unclear return timeline — he could miss most or all of 2026. Yosh Nijman (primary backup) retired. Stone Forsythe (1yr/$2M, PFF 55.2 grade) is not a reliable starter. The Panthers are highly vulnerable at LT specifically. OT should ESCALATE to tier-1 draft priority. Taylor Moton is solid at RT, but LT cover is critical. Drafting a tackle at pick 19 is a genuine option.

Rico Dowdle (back-to-back 1,000-yard seasons) departed to Pittsburgh on a 2yr/$12.25M deal. The Panthers did not sign a direct RB replacement in free agency. The remaining room of Chuba Hubbard (lost starting role in 2025 with 3.8 ypc), Jonathan Brooks (injury history, 3rd-year developing), and Trevor Etienne is uncertain. RB escalates from a background need to a meaningful draft consideration in rounds 2-4. Not a Day 1 pick, but warrants attention.

A'Shawn Robinson cut (cap savings) thins the IDL rotation behind Derrick Brown. LaBryan Ray re-sign provides some continuity but is a minimum-tier deal. Brown is healthy and the cornerstone; behind him the depth is thin. IDL need remains at tier-3 — a mid-round draft add would be prudent but it's not urgent.

Kenny Pickett on a 1yr/$7.5M deal gives Bryce Young a capable (if unspectacular) backup with starting experience. Andy Dalton (38) is expected to be released. QB depth improves from a concern (aging Dalton) to a reasonable backup situation with Pickett. Still a 1-year deal, so QB depth could re-enter consideration in Day 3 of the draft, but it's no longer a priority.

No major WR departures. Panthers return all four of their top WRs from 2025: Tetairoa McMillan (2025 Offensive Rookie of the Year candidate), Jalen Coker (tendered), Xavier Legette, and Brycen Tremayne (tendered). John Metchie III (1yr) adds depth with Bryce Young familiarity. WR is a non-need in the draft.

key_additions.headline: Panthers' largest EDGE deal ever; 61 QB pressures in 2025 gives Evero's defense the elite pass rusher it has desperately lacked
2025 Second-Team All-Pro and 5-INT linebacker fulfills GM Morgan's mission to 'attack' the position — Carolina's best LB addition in years
Former first-round pick with 27 NFL starts replaces aging Andy Dalton as Bryce Young's backup
Bridge center arrives to fill Cade Mays' void; ranked 17th among qualifying centers by PFF in 2025 (8th-lowest QB pressure rate)
Swing tackle depth signing to cover Ikem Ekwonu's uncertain recovery timeline from torn patellar tendon
Bryce Young's Alabama teammate reunites in Carolina as WR4 depth and potential deep threat

key_losses.headline: Back-to-back 1,000-yard rusher departs, leaving an unsettled backfield behind Chuba Hubbard and Jonathan Brooks
Starting center leaves for Detroit, immediately creating a center need that was patched with bridge signing Luke Fortner
Cap casualty release thins IDL rotation behind Derrick Brown after two productive seasons as a Panther
Backup tackle retires, compounding OT depth concern with Ekwonu injured; Panthers answered with Forsythe signing
